gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The signed value of a single trit."] # [must_use] pub fn digit (t : Trit) -> i64 { match t { Trit :: Neg => - 1 , Trit :: Zero => 0 , Trit :: Pos => 1 , } }
fn from_digit (d : i64) -> Trit { match d { - 1 => Trit :: Neg , 1 => Trit :: Pos , 0 => Trit :: Zero , _ => { debug_assert ! (false , "balanced-ternary digit out of range: {d}") ; Trit :: Zero } } }
gaps.reason: function body contains a macro-invocation statement (e.g. `debug_assert!`/`println!`) — no macro system in this grammar fragment

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Balanced full-adder over single trits: returns `(digit_out, carry_out)` with the exact invariant"] # [doc = " `digit(a) + digit(b) + digit(carry_in) == digit(digit_out) + 3·digit(carry_out)`. The sum"] # [doc = " `s = digit(a)+digit(b)+digit(carry_in) ∈ [−3, 3]`, and `(s+1).rem_euclid(3)−1` / `(s+1).div_euclid(3)`"] # [doc = " are provably balanced trits, so both outputs are in `{−1, 0, +1}`. This is the **single**"] # [doc = " never-silent digit primitive both the fixed-width [`add`] and the growable [`BigTernary`] ripple"] # [doc = " (DRY); it is exhaustively oracle-tested over all 27 inputs (`add_with_carry_is_exhaustively_correct`)."] # [doc = " Guarantee: **Exact** (C2)."] # [must_use] pub (crate) fn add_with_carry (a : Trit , b : Trit , carry_in : Trit) -> (Trit , Trit) { let s = digit (a) + digit (b) + digit (carry_in) ; let d = (s + 1) . rem_euclid (3) - 1 ; let c = (s + 1) . div_euclid (3) ; (from_digit (d) , from_digit (c)) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The unique `m`-trit balanced representation of `value`, MSB-first — or `None` if `value` lies"] # [doc = " outside the `m`-trit range (an explicit out-of-range result, never a silent truncation; §3.1)."] # [must_use] pub fn int_to_trits (value : i64 , m : u32) -> Option < Vec < Trit > > { let mut v = value ; let mut lsb_first = Vec :: with_capacity (m as usize) ; for _ in 0 .. m { let mut r = v . rem_euclid (3) ; v = v . div_euclid (3) ; if r == 2 { r = - 1 ; v += 1 ; } lsb_first . push (from_digit (r)) ; } if v != 0 { return None ; } lsb_first . reverse () ; Some (lsb_first) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Ripple-carry add over two equal-length MSB-first trit strings, fixed-width. Returns `None` on"] # [doc = " overflow (a non-zero final carry), i.e. when the true sum leaves the `m`-trit range — explicit,"] # [doc = " never a silent wrap."] # [must_use] pub fn add (a : & [Trit] , b : & [Trit]) -> Option < Vec < Trit > > { if a . len () != b . len () { return None ; } let m = a . len () ; let mut out = vec ! [Trit :: Zero ; m] ; let mut carry = Trit :: Zero ; for i in (0 .. m) . rev () { let (d , c) = add_with_carry (a [i] , b [i] , carry) ; out [i] = d ; carry = c ; } if carry != Trit :: Zero { return None ; } Some (out) }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Fixed-width multiplication. Computes the full product by shifted accumulation (independent of"] # [doc = " machine integer multiply) in a `2m`-trit buffer, then returns the low `m` trits iff the high"] # [doc = " trits are all zero — otherwise `None` (overflow, explicit)."] # [must_use] pub fn mul (a : & [Trit] , b : & [Trit]) -> Option < Vec < Trit > > { if a . len () != b . len () { return None ; } let m = a . len () ; if m == 0 { return Some (Vec :: new ()) ; } let wide = 2 * m ; let mut acc = vec ! [Trit :: Zero ; wide] ; for (k , & bk) in b . iter () . rev () . enumerate () { let factor = digit (bk) ; if factor == 0 { continue ; } let a_signed : Vec < Trit > = if factor < 0 { neg (a) } else { a . to_vec () } ; let mut partial_lsb = vec ! [Trit :: Zero ; wide] ; for (j , & t) in a_signed . iter () . rev () . enumerate () { partial_lsb [k + j] = t ; } let mut acc_msb : Vec < Trit > = acc . iter () . rev () . copied () . collect () ; let partial_msb : Vec < Trit > = partial_lsb . iter () . rev () . copied () . collect () ; acc_msb = add (& acc_msb , & partial_msb) ? ; acc = acc_msb . iter () . rev () . copied () . collect () ; } if acc [m ..] . iter () . any (| & t | t != Trit :: Zero) { return None ; } let low_msb : Vec < Trit > = acc [.. m] . iter () . rev () . copied () . collect () ; Some (low_msb) }
gaps.reason: function body has a semicolon-terminated (value-discarding) statement expression before the tail — a `let`-chain body maps only simple `let x = e;` bindings plus a single trailing expression

gaps.snippet: # [cfg (test)] mod tests { use super :: * ; # [doc = " **Exhaustive truth-table proof** of the shared balanced full-adder over all 27 inputs: the"] # [doc = " digit identity `a + b + carry_in == digit_out + 3·carry_out` holds exactly. This is the"] # [doc = " regression guard for the DRY extraction — both [`add`] and [`BigTernary`] ripple this one"] # [doc = " primitive, so a broken row fails here immediately (alongside `add_matches_integer_oracle`)."] # [test] fn add_with_carry_is_exhaustively_correct () { for a in [Trit :: Neg , Trit :: Zero , Trit :: Pos] { for b in [Trit :: Neg , Trit :: Zero , Trit :: Pos] { for c in [Trit :: Neg , Trit :: Zero , Trit :: Pos] { let (d , carry) = add_with_carry (a , b , c) ; assert_eq ! (digit (a) + digit (b) + digit (c) , digit (d) + 3 * digit (carry) , "full-adder identity for ({a:?}, {b:?}, {c:?})") ; } } } } # [doc = " Walk every integer representable in `m` trits, paired with its codec encoding."] fn each_in_range (m : u32 , mut f : impl FnMut (i64 , Vec < Trit >)) { let max = max_magnitude (m) . unwrap () ; for v in - max ..= max { f (v , int_to_trits (v , m) . expect ("in range")) ; } } # [test] fn worked_example_matches_spec () { let t = int_to_trits (- 78 , 6) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (t , vec ! [Trit :: Zero , Trit :: Neg , Trit :: Zero , Trit :: Zero , Trit :: Pos , Trit :: Zero]) ; assert_eq ! (trits_to_int (& t) , - 78) ; } # [test] fn range_is_symmetric () { assert_eq ! (max_magnitude (1) , Some (1)) ; assert_eq ! (max_magnitude (6) , Some (364)) ; assert_eq ! (int_to_trits (365 , 6) , None) ; assert_eq ! (int_to_trits (- 365 , 6) , None) ; } # [test] fn codec_round_trips_exhaustively () { for m in 1 ..= 5 { each_in_range (m , | v , t | { assert_eq ! (t . len () , m as usize) ; assert_eq ! (trits_to_int (& t) , v , "round-trip at m={m}") ; }) ; } } # [test] fn neg_is_value_negation () { for m in 1 ..= 5 { each_in_range (m , | v , t | { assert_eq ! (trits_to_int (& neg (& t)) , - v , "neg at m={m}") ; }) ; } } # [doc = " **Oracle property test (add):** the digit-wise ripple-carry adder agrees with the `i64`"] # [doc = " oracle for *every* pair at small widths — in range it equals the encoded sum, out of range"] # [doc = " it is `None`."] # [test] fn add_matches_integer_oracle () { for m in 1 ..= 4 { let max = max_magnitude (m) . unwrap () ; for x in - max ..= max { for y in - max ..= max { let a = int_to_trits (x , m) . unwrap () ; let b = int_to_trits (y , m) . unwrap () ; let got = add (& a , & b) ; let expected = x + y ; if expected . abs () <= max { assert_eq ! (got , int_to_trits (expected , m) , "add {x}+{y} at m={m}") ; } else { assert_eq ! (got , None , "add {x}+{y} should overflow at m={m}") ; } } } } } # [test] fn sub_matches_integer_oracle () { for m in 1 ..= 4 { let max = max_magnitude (m) . unwrap () ; for x in - max ..= max { for y in - max ..= max { let a = int_to_trits (x , m) . unwrap () ; let b = int_to_trits (y , m) . unwrap () ; let got = sub (& a , & b) ; let expected = x - y ; if expected . abs () <= max { assert_eq ! (got , int_to_trits (expected , m) , "sub {x}-{y} at m={m}") ; } else { assert_eq ! (got , None , "sub {x}-{y} should overflow at m={m}") ; } } } } } # [doc = " **Oracle property test (mul):** the shifted-add multiplier agrees with the `i64` oracle for"] # [doc = " every pair at small widths."] # [test] fn mul_matches_integer_oracle () { for m in 1 ..= 4 { let max = max_magnitude (m) . unwrap () ; for x in - max ..= max { for y in - max ..= max { let a = int_to_trits (x , m) . unwrap () ; let b = int_to_trits (y , m) . unwrap () ; let got = mul (& a , & b) ; let expected = x * y ; if expected . abs () <= max { assert_eq ! (got , int_to_trits (expected , m) , "mul {x}*{y} at m={m}") ; } else { assert_eq ! (got , None , "mul {x}*{y} should overflow at m={m}") ; } } } } } # [test] fn unequal_widths_are_rejected () { let a = int_to_trits (1 , 2) . unwrap () ; let b = int_to_trits (1 , 3) . unwrap () ; assert_eq ! (add (& a , & b) , None) ; assert_eq ! (sub (& a , & b) , None) ; assert_eq ! (mul (& a , & b) , None) ; } }
